Precaution_1: Keep the affected area clean and completely dry after washing, as moisture encourages fungal growth
Precaution_2: Apply the prescribed antifungal cream consistently for the full recommended duration, even if symptoms improve early
Precaution_3: Wear loose, breathable cotton clothing and change socks and underwear daily
Precaution_4: Avoid sharing towels, footwear, or personal items with others to prevent spreading the infection

Precaution_1: Identify your specific allergen triggers and take practical steps to minimise exposure at home and outdoors
Precaution_2: Always carry your prescribed antihistamine or emergency medication when going out
Precaution_3: Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons and use air purifiers indoors if needed
Precaution_4: Inform your doctor of all medications and supplements you take, as some can worsen allergic reactions

Precaution_1: Avoid spicy, fatty, and acidic foods, as well as coffee and alcohol, which relax the oesophageal valve
Precaution_2: Eat smaller, more frequent meals and avoid eating within 2-3 hours of bedtime
Precaution_3: Maintain a healthy weight, as excess abdominal weight increases reflux pressure
Precaution_4: Elevate the head of your bed slightly and avoid lying flat immediately after meals

Precaution_1: Take all prescribed medications consistently and attend regular liver function monitoring appointments
Precaution_2: Avoid fatty, fried, and heavily processed foods that are difficult to digest without adequate bile
Precaution_3: Avoid alcohol completely, as it places additional strain on an already compromised liver
Precaution_4: Report any new or worsening symptoms such as increased itching or deepening jaundice to your doctor promptly

Precaution_1: Stop the suspected medication immediately and contact your doctor or pharmacist for guidance
Precaution_2: Never restart a medication that has previously caused a reaction without explicit medical advice
Precaution_3: Always inform every healthcare provider about your known drug reactions before receiving any new treatment
Precaution_4: Seek prompt medical attention if you experience swelling, difficulty breathing, or a widespread rash

Precaution_1: Avoid spicy, acidic, and fried foods, as well as caffeine and alcohol, which irritate the stomach lining
Precaution_2: Take all prescribed medications — including antibiotics for H. pylori — for the full course without stopping early
Precaution_3: Avoid non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) like ibuprofen unless specifically advised by your doctor
Precaution_4: Manage stress through relaxation techniques, as stress can worsen ulcer symptoms

Precaution_1: Take antiretroviral therapy (ART) consistently every day without missing doses — this is the cornerstone of management
Precaution_2: Attend all scheduled medical appointments for monitoring and to catch any complications early
Precaution_3: Practice safe hygiene and food safety to protect against opportunistic infections
Precaution_4: Maintain open communication with your healthcare team about any new symptoms or side effects

Precaution_1: Monitor your blood sugar levels regularly as advised and keep a log to share with your doctor
Precaution_2: Take all diabetes medications or insulin exactly as prescribed and at consistent times
Precaution_3: Inspect your feet daily for cuts, blisters, or sores, as diabetes can reduce sensation in the feet
Precaution_4: Attend regular check-ups for eyes, kidneys, and blood pressure, which can be affected by long-term diabetes

Precaution_1: Drink oral rehydration solution (ORS) frequently to replace fluids and electrolytes lost through vomiting and diarrhoea
Precaution_2: Wash hands thoroughly with soap before eating and after using the toilet to prevent spreading the infection
Precaution_3: Avoid solid foods during the acute phase and reintroduce bland foods gradually as symptoms improve
Precaution_4: Seek medical attention if symptoms persist beyond 3 days, or if you notice blood in stools or signs of dehydration

Precaution_1: Always carry your reliever inhaler and ensure it is not expired
Precaution_2: Use your preventer inhaler daily as prescribed, even when you feel well — it reduces underlying inflammation
Precaution_3: Identify and avoid your personal asthma triggers such as dust, smoke, cold air, or specific allergens
Precaution_4: Have a written asthma action plan from your doctor so you know exactly what to do during an episode

Precaution_1: Monitor your blood pressure at home regularly and keep a record to share with your doctor
Precaution_2: Reduce salt intake significantly — avoid processed foods, pickles, and adding extra salt at the table
Precaution_3: Take blood pressure medications consistently every day, even when you feel well
Precaution_4: Manage stress through regular exercise, adequate sleep, and relaxation techniques

Precaution_1: Keep a migraine diary to identify your personal triggers — common ones include stress, poor sleep, certain foods, and dehydration
Precaution_2: Take prescribed migraine medication at the very first sign of an attack for best effect
Precaution_3: Maintain a consistent sleep schedule and avoid skipping meals, both of which are common triggers
Precaution_4: Reduce screen time and ensure your workspace has adequate, non-glaring lighting

Precaution_1: Maintain good posture at all times, especially when sitting at a desk or using a phone or computer
Precaution_2: Use a supportive pillow that keeps your neck in a neutral position while sleeping
Precaution_3: Take regular breaks from screens and perform gentle neck stretches every 30-45 minutes
Precaution_4: Consult a physiotherapist for a personalised exercise programme to strengthen neck muscles safely

Precaution_1: Follow your physiotherapy and rehabilitation programme consistently — regular sessions are essential for recovery
Precaution_2: Ensure your home environment is safe and adapted to your current mobility needs to prevent falls
Precaution_3: Take all prescribed medications including blood pressure and anticoagulant drugs without missing doses
Precaution_4: Attend all specialist follow-up appointments to monitor recovery and adjust treatment as needed

Precaution_1: Drink only clean, boiled, or purified water and avoid raw or street food during recovery
Precaution_2: Avoid alcohol completely — even small amounts can significantly worsen liver function
Precaution_3: Rest adequately and avoid physical exertion until your doctor confirms liver function has normalised
Precaution_4: Attend follow-up blood tests to monitor bilirubin and liver enzyme levels during recovery

Precaution_1: Consult a doctor promptly if you develop fever after travelling to or living in a malaria-prone area
Precaution_2: Use mosquito nets treated with insecticide when sleeping, especially between dusk and dawn
Precaution_3: Apply mosquito repellent to exposed skin and wear long-sleeved clothing in the evenings
Precaution_4: Complete the full course of prescribed antimalarial medication even if you feel better before finishing

Precaution_1: Avoid scratching blisters — keep nails trimmed short and wear cotton gloves at night if needed to prevent scarring and infection
Precaution_2: Stay at home and avoid contact with others, especially pregnant women, newborns, and immunocompromised individuals, until all blisters have crusted over
Precaution_3: Maintain good hygiene — bathe daily with gentle soap and change clothing and bedding regularly
Precaution_4: Consult your doctor if fever is very high, blisters become infected, or symptoms worsen significantly

Precaution_1: Use mosquito repellent and wear full-sleeved clothing, especially during the day when dengue mosquitoes are most active
Precaution_2: Eliminate standing water around your home — flower pots, buckets, and tyres are common breeding sites
Precaution_3: Monitor for warning signs such as severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, or unusual bleeding, and seek care immediately if these occur
Precaution_4: Rest completely and stay well hydrated — avoid aspirin and ibuprofen which can worsen bleeding risk

Precaution_1: Drink only boiled or bottled water and avoid ice from unknown sources
Precaution_2: Avoid eating raw vegetables, salads, and street food during and after recovery
Precaution_3: Complete the full antibiotic course as prescribed, even if you feel better before finishing
Precaution_4: Wash hands thoroughly before eating and after using the toilet, and maintain strict food hygiene at home

Precaution_1: Wash hands thoroughly with soap and water before eating and after using the toilet
Precaution_2: Avoid consuming raw shellfish, uncooked food, or water from unknown sources
Precaution_3: Avoid alcohol completely during recovery and for several months afterwards
Precaution_4: Inform close contacts so they can consider vaccination, as Hepatitis A is preventable with a vaccine

Precaution_1: If not already infected, get vaccinated — the Hepatitis B vaccine is safe and highly effective
Precaution_2: Avoid alcohol completely, as it accelerates liver damage in people with Hepatitis B
Precaution_3: Attend regular liver function monitoring appointments as advised by your doctor
Precaution_4: Practice safe behaviours to prevent transmission — do not share needles, razors, or toothbrushes

Precaution_1: Avoid alcohol entirely, as it significantly accelerates liver damage in Hepatitis C
Precaution_2: Take your prescribed antiviral medication consistently for the full treatment course
Precaution_3: Attend regular monitoring appointments to track treatment response and liver health
Precaution_4: Do not share needles, syringes, or any equipment that may contact blood

Precaution_1: Follow your doctor's treatment plan consistently — Hepatitis D is managed alongside Hepatitis B treatment
Precaution_2: Avoid alcohol completely to protect the liver from additional damage
Precaution_3: Attend regular liver function tests and specialist appointments
Precaution_4: Practice safe behaviours to prevent transmission to others

Precaution_1: Drink only clean, boiled, or purified water — Hepatitis E is primarily spread through contaminated water
Precaution_2: Avoid raw or undercooked food, especially in areas with poor sanitation
Precaution_3: Rest adequately and avoid alcohol during recovery
Precaution_4: Pregnant women should seek prompt medical attention if Hepatitis E is suspected, as it can be more serious during pregnancy

Precaution_1: Stop alcohol consumption completely and immediately — this is the single most important step
Precaution_2: Seek support for alcohol cessation through your doctor, counsellor, or a support group
Precaution_3: Eat a nutritious, protein-rich diet to support liver repair
Precaution_4: Attend all medical follow-up appointments to monitor liver recovery and adjust treatment

Precaution_1: Cover your mouth and nose with a tissue or your elbow when coughing or sneezing, and dispose of tissues safely
Precaution_2: Take all TB medications exactly as prescribed for the full duration — stopping early leads to drug resistance
Precaution_3: Ensure your living space is well-ventilated to reduce the risk of spreading TB to household members
Precaution_4: Inform close contacts so they can be tested and treated if necessary

Precaution_1: Rest at home for the first 2-3 days to allow your immune system to fight the infection
Precaution_2: Wash hands frequently and avoid touching your face to prevent spreading the virus
Precaution_3: Drink warm fluids like herbal teas and broths to soothe the throat and stay hydrated
Precaution_4: Avoid close contact with vulnerable individuals such as the elderly, infants, and immunocompromised people while symptomatic

Precaution_1: Take the full course of prescribed antibiotics without stopping early, even if you feel better
Precaution_2: Rest completely and avoid returning to work or strenuous activity until your doctor advises it is safe
Precaution_3: Stay well hydrated and eat nutritious meals to support immune recovery
Precaution_4: Seek immediate medical attention if breathing becomes significantly worse, lips turn bluish, or confusion develops

Precaution_1: Eat a high-fibre diet and drink plenty of water daily to keep stools soft and prevent straining
Precaution_2: Never ignore the urge to have a bowel movement — delaying increases straining and worsens piles
Precaution_3: Take warm sitz baths for 10-15 minutes after bowel movements to soothe discomfort
Precaution_4: Avoid prolonged sitting on the toilet and do not strain — if a bowel movement does not come easily, try again later

Precaution_1: Call emergency services immediately if you experience chest pain, breathlessness, or pain radiating to the arm or jaw
Precaution_2: Take prescribed medications including aspirin, statins, and blood pressure drugs consistently every day
Precaution_3: Follow your cardiac rehabilitation programme and attend all follow-up appointments
Precaution_4: Make lasting lifestyle changes — quit smoking, eat a heart-healthy diet, and exercise regularly as advised

Precaution_1: Avoid standing or sitting in one position for long periods — move and flex your legs regularly throughout the day
Precaution_2: Wear compression stockings as recommended by your doctor, especially during long periods of standing or travel
Precaution_3: Elevate your legs above heart level for 15-20 minutes several times a day to reduce swelling
Precaution_4: Maintain a healthy weight and exercise regularly to improve circulation and reduce vein pressure

Precaution_1: Take your thyroid hormone medication at the same time every morning on an empty stomach for consistent absorption
Precaution_2: Attend regular blood tests to monitor thyroid hormone levels and adjust dosage as needed
Precaution_3: Avoid taking thyroid medication alongside calcium supplements, iron, or antacids — space them by at least 4 hours
Precaution_4: Do not adjust your medication dose without consulting your doctor, even if symptoms change

Precaution_1: Take all prescribed thyroid medications consistently and do not stop without medical advice
Precaution_2: Avoid iodine-rich foods and supplements, as excess iodine can stimulate the thyroid further
Precaution_3: Attend regular thyroid function tests to monitor treatment response
Precaution_4: Manage stress and avoid stimulants like caffeine which can worsen palpitations and anxiety

Precaution_1: Never skip meals or go for long periods without eating — eat regular, balanced meals every 3-4 hours
Precaution_2: Always carry a fast-acting sugar source such as glucose tablets, fruit juice, or sweets
Precaution_3: Monitor blood sugar levels regularly, especially before driving or exercising
Precaution_4: Inform family members, colleagues, and close friends about your condition so they can assist during an episode

Precaution_1: Maintain a healthy weight to reduce mechanical stress on affected joints
Precaution_2: Exercise regularly with low-impact activities — staying active is one of the best ways to manage osteoarthritis
Precaution_3: Use hot or cold packs on affected joints to manage pain and stiffness as needed
Precaution_4: Consult a physiotherapist for a personalised exercise plan and advice on joint protection techniques

Precaution_1: Exercise regularly with gentle, joint-friendly activities to maintain mobility and reduce stiffness
Precaution_2: Maintain a healthy weight to reduce stress on joints
Precaution_3: Take prescribed medications consistently and attend regular rheumatology or GP follow-ups
Precaution_4: Use assistive devices or joint supports if recommended to protect joints during daily activities

Precaution_1: Move slowly and deliberately when changing positions — sit on the edge of the bed before standing
Precaution_2: Ask your doctor or physiotherapist about the Epley manoeuvre, which can resolve BPPV in one or two sessions
Precaution_3: Avoid sudden head movements and activities that trigger dizziness until symptoms resolve
Precaution_4: Consult your doctor if episodes are frequent, prolonged, or accompanied by hearing loss or severe headache

Precaution_1: Cleanse your face gently twice daily with a mild, non-comedogenic cleanser — avoid harsh scrubbing
Precaution_2: Do not squeeze or pick at pimples, as this worsens inflammation and increases the risk of scarring
Precaution_3: Use only non-comedogenic, oil-free skincare and makeup products
Precaution_4: Apply prescribed topical treatments consistently and be patient — most acne treatments take 6-8 weeks to show results

Precaution_1: Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily to flush bacteria from the urinary tract
Precaution_2: Urinate promptly when you feel the urge and always urinate after sexual activity
Precaution_3: Maintain thorough personal hygiene — always wipe front to back after using the toilet
Precaution_4: Complete the full course of prescribed antibiotics even if symptoms resolve within a day or two

Precaution_1: Keep skin well moisturised at all times — apply emollient immediately after bathing while skin is still slightly damp
Precaution_2: Avoid known triggers such as stress, alcohol, smoking, and skin injuries
Precaution_3: Use prescribed topical treatments consistently and attend regular dermatology follow-ups
Precaution_4: Protect skin from sunburn — while moderate sun exposure can help, burning triggers flare-ups

Precaution_1: Apply prescribed antibiotic cream or take oral antibiotics exactly as directed for the full course
Precaution_2: Keep sores covered with a clean dressing and avoid touching or scratching the affected area
Precaution_3: Wash hands thoroughly before and after touching the affected area, and wash clothing and bedding separately
Precaution_4: Keep children home from school until sores have crusted over or 48 hours after starting antibiotic treatment
